description: §41.13 parseVariant (b) failure mode — UnknownVariant. The wire value carries a `tag` discriminator ('{"tag":"Archived"}') whose value matches NO variant of LoadResult (Success/Empty/Failed). parseVariant raises ::ParseError::UnknownVariant(tag) and the exhaustive !{} handler routes to the UnknownVariant arm, recording @parseError = "unknown-variant". The contract asserts WHICH ParseError variant fired; it deliberately does NOT assert on the bound `tag` payload — see the impl#1-vs-SPEC divergence note in the ss61 dispatch report (single-field !{} arm binding binds the whole .data object, not the field value).
rationale: §41.13 (Failure type / variant semantics): UnknownVariant(tag) — the JSON discriminator value does not match any variant name in the second-argument enum. Surfaced at runtime via E-PARSEVARIANT-UNKNOWN-VARIANT and matched by the ::UnknownVariant arm inside the failable-call handler. The discriminator key is the enum's own variant names (§41.13 normative statement: no discriminatorKey=/mapping= translation).
